<!-- GENERATED by `bun run gen` from scenarios/postgres/03-locking/lock-timeout.yaml — do not edit by hand. -->

```timeline
A: UPDATE 200 (locks row 1)
B: UPDATE 300 → gives up after 100ms ← 55P03 canceling statement due to lock timeout
A: COMMIT
B: UPDATE 300 (retry succeeds)
```

```transcript
A> BEGIN;
BEGIN

A> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 200 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1

B> SET lock_timeout = '100ms';
SET
```

*B queues for the row lock like anyone else — but gives up after 100ms.*

```transcript
B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 300 WHERE id = 1; -- lock_not_available, raised after the timeout
ERROR:  55P03: canceling statement due to lock timeout
```

*The failure canceled only B's statement — a retry after A commits works.*

```transcript
A> COMMIT;
COMMIT

B> UPDATE accounts SET balance = 300 WHERE id = 1;
UPDATE 1

B> SELECT balance FROM accounts WHERE id = 1;
 balance 
---------
     300 
(1 row)
```

<small>Verified against PostgreSQL 18.4 · [Run it yourself](/about/run-locally) · [Scenario source](https://github.com/svyatov/database-transactions/blob/main/scenarios/postgres/03-locking/lock-timeout.yaml)</small>
